name: Amdahl AI
shortDescription: Seed-stage a16z Speedrun startup building a 'customer-intelligence context engine' that structures scattered GTM data into a cited, source-attributed context layer for AI go-to-market agents.
businessModel: B2B SaaS. Amdahl AI sells an AI 'customer-intelligence context engine' for go-to-market (GTM) teams. It ingests structured data (CRM, analytics) and unstructured natural-language data (call transcripts, support tickets, meeting notes, docs) from across the GTM stack, then structures and enriches it so AI agents and human teams get cited, source-attributed answers instead of hallucinated content. Delivered via integrations plus an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server and a REST API, positioned as 'the data scientist your GTM team never got to hire.' Pricing and revenue model are not publicly disclosed; it appears to be a contract/subscription model. NOTE: founded 2025, ~5 employees — this is an early, thinly-documented company and most figures below are self-reported.
marketContext: Amdahl AI plays in the emerging 'context layer' category for AI-native GTM: the thesis is that scattered, unstructured GTM data is the 'wrong input at the source' for AI agents, so a structuring/semantic layer is the missing piece between raw tools (CRM, call recorders, support) and the agents acting on them. It is named as an homage to computer architect Gene Amdahl (Amdahl's Law) — a deliberate, separate entity from the historical Amdahl Corporation mainframe maker. The competitor list is largely analyst inference: no source explicitly names Amdahl's competitors, and there is no Tier-1 press coverage of the company.

explainer.plain: Amdahl AI is a very young startup (founded 2025, around five people, backed by Andreessen Horowitz's Speedrun program). It builds software that gathers all the messy data a sales-and-marketing team generates — call recordings, CRM records, support tickets, notes — and organizes it into a clean, trustworthy 'context layer.' The pitch is that AI sales agents only work well if they're fed good, structured data with sources attached, and Amdahl provides exactly that layer so the AI's answers can be traced back to where they came from.
explainer.technical: Amdahl AI offers a customer-intelligence context engine that unifies structured (CRM, analytics) and unstructured (calls, support docs, notes) GTM data into a queryable, source-attributed context layer. It exposes that layer to AI agents via an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server and a REST API, and ships use-case workflows for Sales, Sales Enablement, Product Marketing and Content Marketing. The company reports SOC 2 Type II, zero data retention with Anthropic, and no training on customer data. Self-reported (unverified) benchmarks claim it beats standard connectors 8 of 10 questions (4.1/5 vs 2.4/5) with ~400x lower token usage while improving accuracy.
explainer.simple: Think of an AI sales assistant as a new hire who's only as good as the briefing you give it. Most companies' briefings are a mess — scattered across call recordings, spreadsheets, support tickets and Slack. Amdahl AI is the team member who reads all of that and turns it into one clean, organized, footnoted briefing, so the AI assistant gives answers you can actually trust and trace. It's a tiny, brand-new company (founded 2025, ~5 people, backed by a16z's startup program), so almost everything known about it comes from the company itself rather than independent reporting.

differentiation.theyMake: Generic pipes that dump raw records into an LLM
differentiation.amdahlEdge: Amdahl claims to structure and enrich GTM data into a cited context layer — self-reported to beat generic connectors on accuracy with far fewer tokens (unverified).
Positions as a horizontal context layer across the whole GTM stack (and integrates Gong as a source) rather than a single-modality analytics tool.
Listed as an integration, not a head-to-head competitor; Amdahl focuses on unifying first-party context for agents rather than prospect enrichment.
Amdahl is narrowly focused on GTM/revenue context and agent-readiness (MCP) rather than general enterprise knowledge.

customers: ~20 signed design partners (count company-reported; names undisclosed)
Pipeline names cited by company: Instacart, OpenTable, Airwallex, Stripe (prospects, NOT confirmed paying customers)
ipoReadiness: Not applicable near-term. Amdahl AI is a seed-stage company (founded 2025, ~5 employees) backed by a16z Speedrun (program checks up to $1M) with Precursor Ventures also listed. No priced round, revenue, or valuation has been confirmed by any reputable source, and reported customer names (Instacart, OpenTable, Airwallex, Stripe) are pipeline/prospects, not paying customers. An IPO is many years and several orders of magnitude of scale away; the near-term question is product-market fit and converting design partners to paid.

leadership.name: Robert Khoury
leadership.title: Co-Founder & CTO

leadership.name: Arya Soltanieh
leadership.title: Co-Founder & Lead Engineer
leadership.background: Listed as co-founder/lead engineer on the a16z Speedrun profile and company site; specific prior background not individually attributed.

notes: DATA-CONFIDENCE WARNING: Amdahl AI is an early, thinly-documented seed-stage company with NO Tier-1 press coverage. The bulk of the information here is self-reported (company site amdahl.co, a16z Speedrun profile, founder LinkedIn/podcast). No funding dollar amount, valuation, or revenue is confirmed by a reputable source; Crunchbase/PitchBook pages were not accessible. Reported customer names are pipeline/prospects, not confirmed paying customers, and the benchmark claims are company self-reported and unverified. This profile is intentionally distinguished from the historical Amdahl Corporation (the Fujitsu-absorbed mainframe maker). No live market data exists — Amdahl AI is private with no ticker. Treat all figures as directional and verify in diligence.

businessOverview: Amdahl AI (amdahl.co / amdahl.ai) is a seed-stage startup building a 'customer-intelligence context engine' for AI go-to-market teams.
It unifies structured (CRM, analytics) and unstructured (calls, support, docs) GTM data into a cited, source-attributed context layer for AI agents.
Founded in 2025; ~5 employees; HQ San Francisco; a member of a16z Speedrun's SR006 cohort, with Precursor Ventures also listed as an investor.
Founders: Annette Sung (CEO), Robert Khoury (CTO), Arya Soltanieh (lead engineer); the name is an homage to Gene Amdahl, NOT the historical Amdahl Corp.
It is delivered via GTM-stack integrations plus an MCP server and REST API; compliance posture cited as SOC 2 Type II and zero data retention with Anthropic.
Company-reported traction: ~20 signed design partners and a ~$4M sales pipeline naming prospects like Instacart, OpenTable, Airwallex and Stripe (prospects, not paying customers).
CONFIDENCE CAVEAT: there is no Tier-1 press coverage and no confirmed funding amount, valuation, or revenue — most facts here are self-reported and unverified.
It is a private company with no stock ticker and no live market data; figures should be treated as directional pending diligence.
brief.productModel: A customer-intelligence context engine: ingest GTM data from across the stack (Salesforce, HubSpot, Gong, Slack, Apollo, 6sense, Clay and others), structure and enrich it with ML, and expose a queryable, source-attributed 'context layer' to AI agents via an MCP server and REST API. Use-case workflows target Sales, Sales Enablement, Product Marketing and Content Marketing. Pricing is undisclosed; the apparent model is a subscription/contract sold to GTM teams, currently via design partners. Self-reported benchmarks (unverified) claim large accuracy and token-efficiency gains versus standard connectors.

recentNews.headline: Amdahl AI emerges from a16z Speedrun (SR006); self-reports ~20 design partners and ~$4M pipeline
recentNews.whyItMatters: The company's launch/positioning is the primary public signal of existence and early traction — but the figures are self-reported via its a16z Speedrun listing and site, with no independent Tier-1 confirmation, so they should be treated as directional marketing claims, not verified metrics.

keyRisks: Information risk: no Tier-1 press coverage; nearly all data is self-reported, so the entire profile carries high uncertainty.
Stage risk: seed-stage, ~5 people, founded 2025 — high failure base rate; no confirmed priced round, revenue, or valuation.
Traction quality: the ~$4M 'pipeline' and named logos (Instacart, OpenTable, Airwallex, Stripe) are prospects, not paying customers; design-partner-to-paid conversion is unproven.

Crowded, fast-moving category: the 'context layer for AI agents' space is filling quickly (RAG connectors, revenue-intelligence incumbents like Gong, enterprise-context players like Glean), with low switching costs.
Platform dependence: relies on third-party GTM tools (CRM, Gong, Slack) and on LLM providers (Anthropic) for the underlying intelligence.
Defensibility: an MCP/connector-plus-structuring layer may be commoditized by platform vendors or model providers shipping native context tooling.
Key-person concentration: a three-founder, ~5-person team; execution hinges on a tiny group.

comps.name: Amdahl AI
comps.note: Seed-stage GTM context-layer for AI agents; ~5 people, a16z Speedrun; valuation undisclosed

comps.name: Clay
comps.note: Well-funded GTM data/enrichment platform; adjacent (and an Amdahl integration) rather than a direct head-to-head

comps.name: Gong
comps.note: Incumbent conversation-analytics platform; single-modality but a natural data source/competitor for context

comps.name: Glean
comps.note: Company-wide enterprise-context assistant; broader scope, validates the 'context layer' thesis at scale

comps.name: Generic RAG connectors
comps.note: The 'do-nothing' alternative Amdahl benchmarks against; low cost, low structure

slideBullets: Seed-stage (2025) a16z Speedrun startup building a cited 'context layer' that feeds clean, source-attributed GTM data to AI agents.
Founders Annette Sung (CEO), Robert Khoury (CTO), Arya Soltanieh; ~5 people; named after Gene Amdahl (not the historical Amdahl Corp).
Delivered via GTM integrations + MCP server + REST API; cites SOC 2 Type II and zero data retention with Anthropic.
Company-reported ~20 design partners and ~$4M pipeline (Instacart, OpenTable, Airwallex, Stripe) — prospects, not paying customers.
CONFIDENCE CAVEAT: no Tier-1 coverage, no confirmed funding/valuation/revenue; private, no ticker — verify everything in diligence.
diligenceQuestions: What is the actual funding to date — is there a priced seed round beyond the a16z Speedrun check, at what amount and valuation, and who are the confirmed investors on the cap table?
Of the ~20 'design partners' and the named pipeline (Instacart, OpenTable, Airwallex, Stripe), how many are paying, at what ACV, and what is retention/usage so far?

What is the moat against platform/model vendors (Salesforce, Gong, Anthropic/OpenAI) shipping native context tooling — is the defensibility in data structuring, integrations, or workflow, and can the self-reported accuracy/token benchmarks be independently reproduced?
